DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS AIDAN FITZSIMON AND STEPHEN FITZSIMON'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS AND MONETARY SANCTIONS. NOT ARGUED/CONTESTED. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. NO OPPOSITION FILED. TERMINATING SANCTIONS ARE GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS REPEATEDLY FAILED TO RESPOND TO DISCOVERY AND SCHEDULE DEPOSITIONS ORDERED BY THE COURT. PLAINTIFF HAS ABANDONED THE CASE. PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE. MONETARY SANCTIONS DENIED. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT DAVIDIDAHO14@GMAIL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID MCDONALD. CLERK: R. WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Off Calendar Oct-16-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom):

Samantha Zelezen, Esq, Law Offices of Carmin K. Shaffer, (925) 488-4817, for Defendants.

No other appearances.

DEFENDANTS AIDAN FITZSIMON and STEPHEN FITZSIMON's Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions.

Not argued/contested.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. No opposition filed. Terminating sanctions are granted. The Court finds that Plaintiff has repeatedly failed to respond to discovery and schedule depositions ordered by the Court. Plaintiff has abandoned the case. Plaintiff's complaint is dismissed with prejudice. Monetary sanctions denied.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at davididaho14@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Appear At Deposition; Request For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Samantha J. Zelezen
Off Calendar Mar-28-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: (Zoom)
Samantha Zelezen, Esq. 925-977-1400 Law Offices of Carmin K. Shaffer for Defendant.
No other Appearance.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

RULING - The matter is uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT AIDAN FITZSIMON, STEPHEN FITZSIMON's Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Appear At Deposition; Request For Sanctions.

Defendants' motion appears to be procedurally proper. Defendants gave notice to Plaintiff of the taking of Plaintiff's deposition and Plaintiff failed to appear at the notice time and date. Plaintiff has not filed an opposition to the motion to explain the failure to appear at the noticed deposition. Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.450(c)(1) mandates the imposition of monetary sanctions under the circumstances presented, unless the court find that the person subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of sanctions unjust. No such circumstances appear here, and the amount of sanctions that Defendants request appears reasonable. The Court intends to Grant Defendants' motion and issue the Proposed Order in the form that Defendants provided.

The Court Grants Defendants' motion and will issue the Proposed Order in the form that Defendants will provide.

Ms. Zelezen to subit a proposed order in word format directly to the Judge Pro Tem at tim@ncalegal.com. Counsel to give notice to opposing side once the proposed order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: J. Timothy Nardell, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
